          "parts": [
            "You want the series project set up cleanly so Future You doesn’t wander into a lore thresher. Fine. Here’s the exact setup: the project name, the files to upload, the paste-ready Series_01 charter, and a first-chat prompt that boots Novella 2 and immediately asks for Chapter 1 under the right rails.\n\n---\n\n# 1) Project name (use exactly this)\n\n**Series_01 — Innocent Writer (Novellas 1–5)**\n\nIf you’re allergic to parentheses: **Series_01 • Innocent Writer • Novellas 1–5**. Same thing, different shirt.\n\n---\n\n# 2) Files to upload into the Series_01 project\n\n## Series root\n- `Decisions_Ledger.md`  ← rolling one-liners after each session\n- `N01_Glossary_Final.md`  ← from our last turn\n- `N01_Canon_Locks_v1.0.md`  ← from our last turn\n- `N01_Do_Dont_v1.0.md`  ← from our last turn\n- `N01→N02_Carryover_Packet.md`  ← from our last turn\n- `N01_Manuscript_Final.md`  ← convert from your master: **The Line That Holds.txt**  \n  *(mirror the .txt into .md so your repo is consistent; the .txt is the source of truth)*\n\n## N02 folder (create now; populate before drafting)\n- `N02_Bible.md`\n- `N02_Beats.md`  ← 15 cards\n- `N02_Summary.md`\n- `N02_Canon_Locks.md`  ← provisional rails for drafting\n- `N02_Do_Dont.md`  ← provisional dos/don’ts for drafting\n- `N02_Ch01_Packet.md` … `N02_Ch15_Packet.md`  ← one per chapter\n\nOptional but sensible:\n- `N02_Cast_Sheet.md`\n- `N02_Locations.md`\n\n---\n\n# 3) Paste-ready initial instructions for the entire project (Series_01 charter)\n\nPaste this into the **first message** of the Series_01 project and pin it. This is the only “personality” the Writer should have.\n\n---\n\n## Project Charter: **Series_01 • Novellas 1–5 (Innocent Writer Project)**\n\n**Purpose**  \nThis Custom GPT is the **Innocent Writer** for **Series_01** (five novellas total). It writes clean chapters inside a tightly scoped world:\n- Aesthetic: **glasspunk + forgepunk**, late pre-industrial. **No electricity.**\n- Time: **bells**; civic order via **ridged postings** (icons, bell marks, quill square, ring glyph).\n- Background: **heat pipes exist**; source unknown; **do not investigate**.\n- Magic: **craft-bound** labor: **Set → Trace → Veil → Countermark → Pressure math → Field audit**.\n- Story: **character-driven**, competence and consequence. No cosmic hints. No ledger-in-prose. No thesis lines.\n\n**Series scope & workflow**  \n- Each novella: **15 chapters**, **900–1500 words** each.  \n- POVs: only those named in the current novella’s Bible; one deep-limited POV per chapter unless a Chapter Packet specifies otherwise.  \n- Every chapter ends on a **proof-of-change** image (a physical change in-world).  \n- **Innocence:** model reads only files for the **current novella** plus the relevant **Carryover Packet**. Anything else is **unknown**.\n\n**Files per novella**  \n- `N##_Bible.md`, `N##_Beats.md`, `N##_Summary.md`  \n- `N##_Canon_Locks.md`, `N##_Do_Dont.md`  \n- `Decisions_Ledger.md` (series-level, newest on top)  \n- Per-chapter: `N##_Ch##_Packet.md` with **Available / Restricted / Glossary (10–20)**  \n- Between books: `N##→N##+1_Carryover_Packet.md` (sanitized)\n\n**Output rules (non-negotiable)**  \n1) 900–1500 words. No filler.  \n2) End on a concrete **proof-of-change**.  \n3) Voice: plainspoken, tactile, competence-first.  \n4) **Consent by body effects** only.  \n5) Show rituals via action; no lectures.  \n6) **Bans:** “sing,” “harmonics,” “song,” any sound-as-magic system.  \n7) Ledger lives off-page; use tactile signs only when needed.  \n8) **No god/mask/Auditor/Counterspark.**  \n9) Obey each Packet’s **Available/Restricted**. If prompted past scope:  \n   **“Out of scope for this novella. Provide a Chapter Packet or update the Bible.”**  \n10) Return **chapter text only** unless commentary is explicitly requested.\n\n**Cost signals**  \nUse briefly and physically: **tremor, ear-ring, thirst, breath debt, sleep debt, back/hand strain.**\n\n**Session protocol**  \n1) I provide a **Session Header** and the **Chapter Packet**.  \n2) You return the **chapter only**.  \n3) We optionally pass through an Editor (Surgical).  \n4) We add a one-liner to `Decisions_Ledger.md`.\n\n**Hard refusals**  \n- Gods/Auditor/hidden identities: **“Restricted by innocence protocol for this novella.”**  \n- Outside canon: **“Out of scope for this novella. Provide a Chapter Packet or update the Bible.”**  \n- Analysis instead of chapter: **“Return format for this role is chapter text only.”**\n\n**Quality bar**  \nClear craft choreography; felt cost; photo-clean ending image; no banned words; no ledger in prose; no cosmic breadcrumbs.\n\n---\n\n# 4) First-chat prompt to begin writing **Novella 2** (paste this in the same conversation after the charter)\n\nPaste the block below once you’ve uploaded the N02 files and the carryover. It boots the model straight into Chapter 1 under correct scope.\n\n```\nSESSION: Bootstrap Novella 2\n\nFiles now available to you:\n- N01→N02_Carryover_Packet.md\n- N02_Bible.md\n- N02_Beats.md\n- N02_Summary.md\n- N02_Canon_Locks.md\n- N02_Do_Dont.md\n- N02_Ch01_Packet.md\n\nProtocol reminders:\n- Innocence: you know only the above files.\n- Output: chapter text only, 900–1500 words, end on a physical proof-of-change.\n- Bans: no “sing/harmonics/song,” no ledger-in-prose, no gods/masks/Auditor/Counterspark, no heat-pipe origin digressions.\n- Consent: shown by body effects only.\n\nNovella: N02 — <Insert Title>\nChapter: 01\nPOV: Use the POV indicated in N02_Ch01_Packet.md (deep limited)\nGoal: <Insert clear scene goal from Beats/Packet>\nCraft setpiece: <Insert named craft actions from Packet>\nMust end on: <Insert specific proof-of-change image>\nWord target: 900–1500\n\nInclude the N02_Ch01_Packet contents below your understanding before you write (one short line per item: Available, Restricted, Glossary terms you will use). Then return the chapter only.\n```\n\nIf you want to be extra tidy, drop in a prefilled `N02_Ch01_Packet.md` before you paste that. The model will start drafting immediately like a well-trained mule that only kicks when you try cosmic foreshadowing.\n\nDon’t overthink it. Name the project exactly as above, upload the files exactly as listed, paste the charter, then paste the N02 boot prompt. The rest is just work, which, happily, our little Writer does without complaining. Unlike some people."
          ]
        },

          "reasoning_status": "is_reasoning",
          "reasoning_title": "# Let's start by opening and reading the files as listed.\n# First, we need to check the existence and contents of conversation parts and the onboarding documentation, including the zip file.\n\n# Paths to the files as specified by the user\nconversation_parts = [f\"/mnt/data/conversation_part{i}.md\" for i in range(1, 6)]\nonboarding_file = \"/mnt/data/OmniMeta_SourceLaw_Onboarding.md\"\nnovella_zip = \"/mnt/data/Novella_Companion_OnePagers.zip\"\n\n# Helper function to read .md files\ndef read_md_files(paths):\n    content = {}\n    for path in paths:\n        if os.path.exists(path):\n            with open(path, \"r\") as f:\n                content[path] = f.read()\n    return content\n\n# Helper function to unzip .zip file\ndef unzip_novella(zip_file_path):\n    content = {}\n    if zipfile.is_zipfile(zip_file_path):\n        with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_file_path, 'r') as zip_ref:\n            zip_ref.extractall(\"/mnt/data/novella_temp\")\n            content = {file: open(f\"/mnt/data/novella_temp/{file}\", \"r\").read() for file in zip_ref.namelist()}\n    return content\n\n# Read the conversation part files and onboarding md file\nconversation_data = read_md_files(conversation_parts)\nonboarding_data = read_md_files([onboarding_file])\nnovella_data = unzip_novella(novella_zip)\n\n# Combine all data in one structure to return\nall_data = {\n    \"conversation\": conversation_data,\n    \"onboarding\": onboarding_data,\n    \"novella\": novella_data\n}\n\nall_data\n",

    "185722b2-d896-43b4-b5bb-4dec9a4e10ae": {
      "children": [],
      "id": "185722b2-d896-43b4-b5bb-4dec9a4e10ae",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "Short answer: yes, this approach works. Starting each novella with a plain-spoken “here’s what we’re doing and why” essay gives the model the vibe, the limits, and the target. It aligns far better than dumping a rules compendium at it and hoping for miracles. You talk to it like a collaborator, then hand it the artifacts as backing documents. That keeps tone and intent glued together instead of drifting into Prompt Salad.\n\nSince you want momentum, here’s a drop-in kickoff you can paste into that first chat for Novella 2. It’s written in clean, neutral, conversational prose (not promptese) and assumes the canon from your N01 material.\n\n# Novella 2 — Conversation Kickoff (drop-in)\n\n**North Star.** We’re writing a 15-chapter, ~28–35k novella that continues in the same voice as N01: street-level competence, intimate stakes, exhausted tenderness. The engine is “holding under pressure vs letting go on purpose.” We stay grounded in craft details that feel tactile and costly.\n\n**Where we are.** The market dome holds. The Dregs are in strategic retreat; repairs go underground. Jhace’s right hand is close to permanent loss of function. Tiffani’s stasis work is eating ribs and leaving vein burns. Refugees confirm coordinated doctrines spreading city to city (fire as purity, mercy-collapse, perfection geometry, binding communes). Lists and raids are now policy, not accidents.\n\n**What this book does.**  \n1) Converts survival into organization: distribute technique, build cells, document craft so knowledge survives raids.  \n2) Forces triage into character: Jhace learns to choose what not to hold; Tiffani stops playing lone archivist and actually shares the map and methods.  \n3) Brings one doctrine to the door: grind one ideological antagonist into the plot, not all four.  \n4) Pays off intimacy as strategy: trust and shared vein aren’t romance wallpaper; they are the only scalable tech we have.\n\n**Box we will not break.** No gods on-screen as overt deities; no ledger-as-prose; no magic-as-sound; no mask mythology; no Auditor/Counterspark; no cosmology reveals. We stay in posted-clause reality, one rung above grime.\n\n**Tone guide.** Practical poetry. Tools have verbs. Pain is a receipt, not a melodrama. Dialog is short, specific, competent; tenderness arrives sideways during tasks, not speeches.\n\n**Signature images for this volume.**  \n- Vein-light across glass bones while two people breathe in sync.  \n- A tactile board with names you can feel more than read.  \n- A hand that won’t close, teaching anyway.  \n- Heat-pipe glow under a line of sleeping refugees.  \n- Stasis-lock hairline “cracks in the ribs” mirrored by cracks in a dome.\n\n**Structural spine (15 cards, one line each).**  \n1) Aftermath ritual: the dome holds; a “quiet win” is priced in blood and names.  \n2) Go dark: operations move underground; first crew-wide technique share.  \n3) Pressure test: sabotage pattern appears inside a fix; quality control fails; we adapt.  \n4) Refugee truth: Halrick/Mourne stories; the map becomes “now,” not theory.  \n5) The ask: Jhace agrees to teach instead of hold; Tiffani agrees to show everything.  \n6) First cell stands: distributed crews stabilize two targets in parallel.  \n7) Raid math: the list arrives; someone we trained is taken; we keep going.  \n8) Breakpoint: a repair requires letting a building die to save a family.  \n9) Midpoint setpiece: stasis-plus-shared-vein under hostile eyes; we escape with a ledger, not a trophy.  \n10) Consequence: hand function crosses a line; we retool roles, not the mission.  \n11) Doctrine at the door: a purity sermon over a fireline forces a public stance.  \n12) Community replies: bread, bodies, and breath; the Dregs choose their own compliance.  \n13) Teach the teachers: second ring of instructors take over; we’re now replaceable by design.  \n14) The hard let-go: we abandon an anchor we love to prevent a district-wide failure.  \n15) Proof-of-change: a structure stands because of people we taught, not us; closing image mirrors Ch1 but the weight is shared.\n\n**Hard constraints for drafting.** All craft has cost on-screen. Every scene ends on a physical proof-of-change image. No omniscient lore drops. If something is “clever,” it must be payable in the body or the street.\n\n**Red lines.** No cosmology, no Auditor, no mask play, no sound-as-magic, no prose-ledger. Doctrine figures remain rumor and report, not POV on-stage.\n\n---\n\n# N02 Chapter 1 Packet (filled so drafting can actually start)\n\n- **POV:** Tiffani, close third.  \n- **Goal:** Stand up the first fully independent repair cell and walk away before the last bolt is set. Prove the work can continue without her on the line.  \n- **Available:** Locations: market dome, western alcoves, northern tenement, civic hall exterior; Characters: Jhace, Bram, Kylar, Selenthe, Brighlee, two refugees; Tools/Craft: shared-vein, basic velding pedagogy, triage map; Policy surface: lists and curfew enforcement visible.  \n- **Restricted:** No doctrine leaders on-screen; no mask/Auditor/myth beats; no ledger-in-prose; no magic-sound; no cosmology.  \n- **Craft setpiece:** “Teach, Then Let Go.” Tiffani threads through a novice for three breaths, then snaps contact and makes them finish the redistribution solo while she holds nothing.  \n- **Must end on:** Proof-of-change image: a rib stabilizes from three meters away while Tiffani’s hands are at her sides; the student’s stylus is the only thing glowing.  \n- **Glossary terms in play:** Tempermark, Ring glyph, Shade, Slake, Wedge-before-Veil, Friend line, Counterline, Fray band, HALT, Clinic slip, Double brace, Feed split, Round check, Lot quarantine.\n\n# Notes for the model (keep conversational)\n\n- We care about work shown, not declared. Every fix has breath, timing, and something that can go wrong.  \n- The relationship is competence-forward. Physical closeness is earned by tasks shared, not by speeches.  \n- Antagonism is systems and doctrine, not a moustache. People enforcing it are painfully plausible.  \n- We teach on the page. If a technique appears, it is named, done, and paid for.\n\nIf you want, I can also spin this into the top of your `N02_Bible.md` and `N02_Beats.md` so your files and your kickoff are saying the same thing without fighting each other. No question that this is the cleaner, saner way to start the session."
          ]
        },

    "20754027-ebec-4df6-bbae-a757208a7519": {
      "children": [
        "6452c5bb-2de1-4364-8bc7-034df49f34d1"
      ],
      "id": "20754027-ebec-4df6-bbae-a757208a7519",
      "message": {
