    "fa5447b9-53a8-4463-8956-c12c96544ff2": {
      "children": [
        "0bdf6ef9-b9b3-41c8-9cda-3b2922a5c743"
      ],
      "id": "fa5447b9-53a8-4463-8956-c12c96544ff2",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "file_search",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "tether_quote",
          "domain": "OmniMeta_SourceLaw_Onboarding.md",
          "tether_id": null,
          "text": "fileciteturn0file0\n\n# Omni Meta — Source‑Law Onboarding Packet\n*(Conversation‑only synthesis. No prose. No spoilers beyond what we agreed in chat.)*\n\n## 0) What this is\nUse this as the **one** report you upload to the **Omni Meta** project as its baseline memory. It distills our entire conversation’s decisions, corrections, and rails without dragging in manuscript text. It’s the control tower doc.\n\n---\n\n## 1) Core Decisions (short list you tattoo on your wrist)\n- **Dual aesthetic:** glasspunk + forgepunk. Late pre‑industrial shift; no electricity.\n- **Time:** tracked by **bells**; civic life runs on **ridged postings** (icons, bell marks, quill square, ring glyph).\n- **Heat grid:** every city has heat pipes; source unknown; **do not investigate** yet.\n- **Magic is craft:** actions are the system — **Set → Trace → Veil → Countermark → Pressure math → Field audit**.\n- **Costs show up:** tremor, thirst, ear‑ring, breath/sleep debt, back/hand strain.\n- **Language bans:** no “sing,” “harmonics,” or sound‑as‑magic. Ambient noise is fine, not a grammar.\n- **Prose laws:** no ledger text in narrative; no thesis slogans; consent shown by body response.\n- **Structure:** 15 chapters per novella; **900–1500 words** each; each ends on a **proof‑of‑change image**.\n- **Innocence protocol:** Writers only know local Bible/Beats/Summary + carryover packet. No long‑con/god‑mask in Writer land.\n- **Cosmic curtain:** Only four gods (Xilcore, Blemo, Leesa, Seeri) exist in meta, but public treats them as ideals; **no reveals** in early novellas.\n\n---\n\n## 2) Character anchors (innocent public read)\n- **Jhace** — setter/brast; competence, duty; physical costs are visible; no chosen‑one vibes until the designated break.\n- **Tiffani** — veld/glass; competence first, desire second; reads fully human and mortal until the event in later books.\n- **Crew entrants (family‑seed five):** Brighlee (clerk), Sha’Niah, Valaryn, Chloen, Kylar. Names are fixed; intros paced; each is added in 1s or 2s within first three novellas.\n\n---\n\n## 3) Guardrails we enforced after you yelled (chronology of corrections)\n1. **No listy intros.** Character is revealed through **micro‑actions** that move the task.\n2. **Rituals are shown**, never explained. We killed lines like “the body obeys what the mouth says.”\n3. **No thesis slogans** in prose. Example corpse: “paper moves bodies.”\n4. **Consent beat** is warmth‑then‑tremor‑eases. No meta explanation.\n5. **Ledger language** stays out of the scene. Paper is shown via **tactile signs** (ridged legend, stamp bite).\n6. **Title fix:** “The Line That Sings” → **“The Line That Holds.”**\n7. **Training scenes**: pedagogy through live work, not essays.\n8. **Strict length**: 900–1500 per chapter. Surgical editor removes repetition and meta.\n9. **Innocence**: no god/mask/Auditor hints in early books, even by accident.\n\n---\n\n## 4) Workflow we locked (who does what)\n- **Omni Meta** (this project): All‑knowing architect/auditor. Modes only; never drafts prose.\n- **Series projects**: five **Innocent Writer** GPTs per series (one per novella). Draft only what the packet allows.\n- **Loop per novella**: Finish → **INGEST_FINAL** → **WRAP_PACK** → generate **N+1 Writer/Editor** configs → Draft N+1 with innocent Writer.\n\n---\n\n## 5) Omni Meta — Modes (do not improvise)\n- **INGEST_FINAL** → Update memory with canon deltas and carryover facts.\n- **WRAP_PACK** → Emit 4 files: `Glossary_Final`, `Canon_Locks_v1.0`, `Do_Dont_v1.0`, `N→N+1_Carryover_Packet (Allowed/Restricted/OpenThreads)`.\n- **GENERATE_WRITER_GPT (N+1)** → Full instruction block for the next **Innocent Writer**.\n- **GENERATE_EDITOR_GPT (N+1)** → Full instruction block for the next **Surgical Editor**.\n- **LIBRARIAN_PACKET** → Per‑chapter **Available/Restricted/Glossary** for the Writer.\n- **AUDITOR_CONTINUITY** → PASS/FAIL with pinpoint flags; no rewrites.\n\n**Memory policy:** retain only closed‑book facts; do not recall anything you weren’t fed.\n\n---\n\n## 6) Carryover Packet (sanitized handoff template)\n```md\n# N→N+1_Carryover_Packet\n## Allowed\n- Facts the next book can use (people, tools, civic changes, injuries with cost signals).\n\n## Restricted\n- Long‑con, god/mask, Auditor/Counterspark, hidden identities, any late‑book reveals.\n\n## Open Threads\n- Clean, non‑spoiler threads that can continue civically (council pressure, quotas, heat anomalies without source).\n\n## Glossary (10–20 items)\n- Short, unambiguous definitions that an “innocent” model can deploy safely.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7) Session headers you actually use\n**Bootstrap the next novella (inside Omni Meta):**\n```md\nSESSION: Bootstrap N+1\n\nMODE: WRAP_PACK\nFiles: N_Final.md, N_Canon_Locks.md, N_Do_Dont.md, Decisions_Ledger.md\n→ Return: Glossary_Final, Canon_Locks_v1.0, Do_Dont_v1.0, N→N+1_Carryover_Packet\n\nMODE: GENERATE_WRITER_GPT (N+1)\nFiles: N+1_Bible.md, N→N+1_Carryover_Packet.md\n→ Return: full instructions for N+1 WRITER (Innocent)\n\nMODE: GENERATE_EDITOR_GPT (N+1)\nFiles: N+1_Bible.md\n→ Return: full instructions for N+1 EDITOR (Surgical)\n```\n\n**Close a novella (inside Omni Meta):**\n```md\nSESSION: Close Novella N\n\nMODE: INGEST_FINAL\nFiles: N_Final.md, N_Canon_Locks.md, N_Do_Dont.md, Decisions_Ledger.md\n→ Return: Memory Delta (canonical changes + allowed carryover facts)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8) Misunderstandings we corrected (because, yes, we argued)\n- **Sound language** tried to creep in via titles and metaphors; we banned it from magic vocabulary.\n- **Ledger creep** kept sliding into prose; we moved it to off‑page artifacts and tactile signs only.\n- **Brighlee** oscillated between flavor and prop; solution: either purely functional, or a recurring two‑beat tic — pick one per scene.\n- **Training scenes** tempted lecture mode; we forced live rack fixes and posted icons to teach naturally.\n- **Overlength**: N2 ballooned; the surgical prompt enforces page discipline.\n\n---\n\n## 9) File naming & versioning (so Future‑You doesn’t cry)\n- `N##_Canon_Locks_v1.0.md`, `N##_Do_Dont_v1.0.md` only after the book closes.\n- Carryover name: `N##→N##+1_Carryover_Packet.md`.\n- Rolling ledger: `Decisions_Ledger.md` with newest at top.\n- Writers: never get global archives; only local Bible + Carryover + Packet.\n\n---\n\n## 10) Quick refusal lines (pin these)\n- **Omni Meta** when asked to draft prose: “Not an Omni Meta mode.”\n- **Innocent Writer** when asked for spoilers: “Out of scope for this novella. Provide a Chapter Packet or update the Bible.”\n\n---\n\n## 11) What NOT to upload to Writer projects\n- Our five‑part conversation archives.\n- Any long‑con/god‑mask/Auditor documents.\n- Finished manuscripts from other novellas.\n\n**Congratulations.** You now have the one doc that tells the truth and slaps hands when needed.",
          "title": "OmniMeta_SourceLaw_Onboarding.md",
          "url": "file-9eANCbgfjGuqDHCTFN2XBF"
        },

          "parts": [
            "The file contents provided above are truncated/partial snippets. The complete content for these files IS accessible via querying.\n\nIf the user asks a question related to these files, and the provided snippets do not clearly answer it, you **MUST** use the `file_search` tool to search the full document contents before responding.\n\n## How to invoke the file_search tool\nIf the user's query isn't sufficiently answered by the provided snippets, parsed text, or images, you should invoke `file_search.msearch`.\nTo search the full content of the available files, you **must** send one message with recipient `to=file_search.msearch` in the analysis channel. Your message should be structured exactly as follows:\n```\n{\n  'queries': ['Example query 1', 'Example query 2']  // List of up to five queries to search for.\n}\n```\n\nCarefully balance the number of `file_search` calls and the quality of your response with the latency incurred by `file_search` calls.\n\nImportant guidelines for query formatting:\n- Your message must be a valid JSON object. Do not include any extraneous text, backticks, markdown formatting, or comments beyond what is shown.\n- The `queries` field must be a list of strings.\n- One of the queries **MUST** exactly match the user's original question, but stripped of any extraneous details, and with ambiguous references clearly resolved using context from the conversation. It **MUST** be a complete sentence.\n- Even if you suspect the user meant something else, one query **MUST** still be their original question.\n\n## How to handle results from file_search\nThe file search tool will respond with the relevant search results from the full files. After you receive these results:\n- Carefully evaluate each result to determine its relevance and quality.\n- Use the high-quality and relevant results to inform your answer to the user's question.\n- When possible, reuse information from prior file_search results and avoid repeating identical queries.\n\n## Tool usage instructions and ordering\nWhen the user asks a question, carefully consider whether the provided snippets sufficiently answer the query, whether you need to invoke `file_search.msearch`, or another available tool (such as `web`, if available) should be used instead.\n\nTool-ordering Guidelines:\n1. For queries related to uploaded files, if snippets are insufficient, strongly prefer invoking `file_search.msearch` over other tools.\n    - Avoid using `web.run`, `python` analysis, or any other tools until you have either:\n        - Confirmed with high confidence the answer is not in the files, or\n        - Have high confidence `file_search_tool` queries are not sufficient to answer the query.\n2. Avoid usage of `web_search/web.run` for queries related to uploaded files\n    - Carefully consider whether the user's query is better answered by web search results rather than available snippets or results from `file_search_tool`.\n    - Unless the user's query is clearly unrelated to the uploaded files, prefer using `file_search.msearch`. You can always follow up with a web search if necessary for a more complete response.\n3. Avoid using `python` unless necessary or requested by user.\n    - If the user's query is answerable from the parsed text and images from the uploaded files, or from `file_search_tool`, do not invoke `python`.\n\nAlways keep this instruction in mind for the rest of the conversation, as the user may reference these files or ask related questions later on."
          ]
